text:
Market today: Mood is cautiously risk-on. The real driver is SK Hynix surging 12% on Micron earnings - that's a direct green light for semis and chip names. Hormuz tanker traffic resuming pulls the rug from under any oil-spike fear, keeping macro relatively calm for tech.

Your holdings: Three need attention. PLTR is down 12.77% and flagged SELL - on margin, that hole is already painful and it has no catalyst here. AVGO is your largest position, down 12.45% and flagged TRIM - chip tailwind helps, but size plus loss on margin is a real risk if momentum stalls. SPAL is down 14.48% on a 2x leveraged ETF that decays daily - these bleed fast; know your exit. SPCX is brand-new and extremely volatile with almost no price history - keep size tiny and manage it manually.

Watch: 1) Does AVGO catch a bid off the Micron/Hynix chip wave - that's your clearest near-term read. 2) PLTR's stop at $85 - price is $113.50 but the SELL flag means trend is broken; watch for a lower-high failure.

Bottom line: The chip signal is real but your two biggest losers are on margin - trim before the market decides for you.
